unit_12 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_12

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 258 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 TC TRIPLE
Famous books have been(i) the theater: Dickens suffered so much hackneyed staging that he felt forced to change his writing style, throwing time shifts into his narratives to trip up the adapters. Undeterred, theatrical managements ever since have used other people's literary repute to fill seats, though the adaptations still seldom(ii) their literary models. But in the1990s a small British theater company, Shared Experience, began to (iii) the received wisdom about adaptations by developing thrilling and uniquely theatrical versions of several classic novels.Famous books have been(i) the theater: Dickens suffered so much hackneyed staging that he felt forced to change his writing style, throwing time shifts into his narratives to trip up the adapters. Undeterred, theatrical managements ever since have used other people's literary repute to fill seats, though the adaptations still seldom(ii) their literary models. But in the1990s a small British theater company, Shared Experience, began to (iii) the received wisdom about adaptations by developing thrilling and uniquely theatrical versions of several classic novels.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　much abused by; equaled; confound
disseminatedistortinspired
disseminate: standard unit headword | distort: standard unit headword | inspired: standard unit headword
#2 Sentence Equivalence
His own writing style was ______: colorful and tart in its choice of language, willing to run risks in its allusions, metaphors, and verbal juxtapositions, prone to irreverent conclusions designed to surprise or startle.His own writing style was ______: colorful and tart in its choice of language, willing to run risks in its allusions, metaphors, and verbal juxtapositions, prone to irreverent conclusions designed to surprise or startle.
答案：B, D　audacious; striking
strikestrikinglyricalstartle
strike: standard unit headword | striking: standard unit headword | lyrical: standard unit headword | startle: standard unit headword
#3 TC SINGLE
The message the author conveys is that the bluefin tuna fishery worldwide is being mismanaged and that despite a few __________ signs, such as the partial recovery of stocks in some areas, the immediate future looks bleak.The message the author conveys is that the bluefin tuna fishery worldwide is being mismanaged and that despite a few __________ signs, such as the partial recovery of stocks in some areas, the immediate future looks bleak.
答案：E　propitious
immediateconvey
immediate: standard unit headword | convey: standard unit headword
#4 Text Completion
The essential difference between writing nonfiction and writing fiction is that the artist can (i)______ a completed vision of the world, while the journalist never can, the real world being always (ii)______. Art provides freedom from the bewildering complexities of constant change. Indeed, it is the very (iii)______ of well-wrought fiction that can sometimes make it feel more real than reality.The essential difference between writing nonfiction and writing fiction is that the artist can (i)______ a completed vision of the world, while the journalist never can, the real world being always (ii)______. Art provides freedom from the bewildering complexities of constant change. Indeed, it is the very (iii)______ of well-wrought fiction that can sometimes make it feel more real than reality.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　realize; unfinished; clarity
raritybewilder
rarity: standard unit headword | bewilder: standard unit headword
#5 SE
The Tigris and Euphrates rivers are known for being ____________: they flow with such a low gradient that they meander considerably and throw numerous side branches.The Tigris and Euphrates rivers are known for being ____________: they flow with such a low gradient that they meander considerably and throw numerous side branches.
答案：E, F　convoluted; intricate
pristineconsiderably
pristine: standard unit headword | considerably: standard unit headword

Founding myths (i)______ the equal division of labor in child care for mothers and fathers. As is often the case, however, theoretical commitments are (ii)______ actual practices. In reality, gender-based (iii)______ persists.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　celebrate; incommensurate with; stratification

答案：D, E　civility; comity

Blank 2
Blank 3

Some norms governing authors' dedications of their works are (i) __________ the norms governing ordinary speech (it may be wrong to lie, and thus wrong to lie in a dedication), but other norms are (ii) __________ dedications. For example, one ought not dedicate to a worthy recipient an object that one feels is devoid of value.Some norms governing authors' dedications of their works are (i) __________ the norms governing ordinary speech (it may be wrong to lie, and thus wrong to lie in a dedication), but other norms are (ii) __________ dedications. For example, one ought not dedicate to a worthy recipient an object that one feels is devoid of value.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　indistinct from; particular to
norm
norm: standard unit headword

#12 Text Completion

Thus, when we are considering a role for sleep in human memory consolidation, we are referring to a possible role in the (ii)______ encoding of information and optimizing of recall, not a (iii)______ of sleep for recalling events of the prior day.It is possible for human to go 40 or more hours without sleep and still be able to (i)______ information acquired at the beginning of the sleepless period. Thus, when we are considering a role for sleep in human memory consolidation, we are referring to a possible role in the (ii)______ encoding of information and optimizing of recall, not a (iii)______ of sleep for recalling events of the prior day.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　disgorge; longer-term; requirement
disgorgerefer

#16 TC TRIPLE
Singers of the tropicalist movement in Brazil goaded the government by purposefully invoking stereotypical images of Brazil as a tropical paradise only to (i) ____________ them with pointed references to political and social problems. But (ii) ____________ in giving offense, the tropicalist singers also managed to annoy the Brazilian left, which shared their social concerns. They embraced an irreverent politics and (iii) ____________, epitomized by the coexistence of bossa nova and rock in the same song, that ignored certain leftist calls for folkloric authenticity.Singers of the tropicalist movement in Brazil goaded the government by purposefully invoking stereotypical images of Brazil as a tropical paradise only to (i) ____________ them with pointed references to political and social problems. But (ii) ____________ in giving offense, the tropicalist singers also managed to annoy the Brazilian left, which shared their social concerns. They embraced an irreverent politics and (iii) ____________, epitomized by the coexistence of bossa nova and rock in the same song, that ignored certain leftist calls for folkloric authenticity.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　subvert; ecumenical; a pastiche of styles
reference
reference: standard unit headword

If there are very few authentic ideas in the poems under consideration, there is also a shortage of them in the author's commentary.Perhaps the best one can say of this new book of criticism is that it is scintillatingly (i) __________. The author is adept at dealing with trivial details in the poets he studies, but he is quite (ii) __________ when it comes to illuminating the overall vision. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　shallow; indifferent; poverty

The letters of accomplished poets and novelists are - as often as not - about drinking and infidelity, about sponging and wasting time. Consequently, one's (i) __________ the writer as (ii) __________ can often come in for a rude shock on contemplation of the writer's correspondence: the soul that composed the poems and the self that wrote the letters can seem (iii) __________.Letters present an awkward stumbling block to those who take a high line about literature as a custodian of moral values. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　reverence for; exemplary; irreconcilable

composed: standard unit headword

Blank 3

Indeed, by the 1920s Washington had become such (ii)______ personage that inevitably someone had to go to the other extreme and try to (iii)______ the legend.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　laudatory; a deified; debunk

exemplify: standard unit headword
#52 Sentence Equivalence
Although Wynne claims to recognize that ______ evidence is available to make definitive statements, she offers them nonetheless, arriving at some sweeping generalizations.Although Wynne claims to recognize that ______ evidence is available to make definitive statements, she offers them nonetheless, arriving at some sweeping generalizations.
答案：C, F　insufficient; meager
meager
meager: standard unit headword
#53 Text Completion
Cynics will dismiss the race between Richard and Gorman as two equally dull candidates. However, the notion that the two leaders are (i)______ does not meet them, for they are as different as can be. Richard is (ii)______ and is fastidious of her appearance. So one could hardly accuse her of being either taciturn or (iii)______ .Cynics will dismiss the race between Richard and Gorman as two equally dull candidates. However, the notion that the two leaders are (i)______ does not meet them, for they are as different as can be. Richard is (ii)______ and is fastidious of her appearance. So one could hardly accuse her of being either taciturn or (iii)______ .
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　interchangeable; loquacious; unkempt
taciturn
taciturn: standard unit headword
#54 Sentence Equivalence
In American art the line between the good and the goods is not a hard-and-fast boundary, for in a commercial society the membrane that separates spirit and store is always ______.In American art the line between the good and the goods is not a hard-and-fast boundary, for in a commercial society the membrane that separates spirit and store is always ______.
答案：A, C　porous; permeable
membrane
membrane: standard unit headword
#55 Text Completion
Recent scholarship has questioned the (i)______ of tropical forests around the world. Archeologists have shown, for example, that the largest contiguous tract of what was thought to be virgin rain forest in the southern Amazon had been transformed into a cultural parkland before European contact, and many of the forest islands in West Africa’s savanna-forest transition zone are (ii)______ as well.Recent scholarship has questioned the (i)______ of tropical forests around the world. Archeologists have shown, for example, that the largest contiguous tract of what was thought to be virgin rain forest in the southern Amazon had been transformed into a cultural parkland before European contact, and many of the forest islands in West Africa’s savanna-forest transition zone are (ii)______ as well.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　naturalness; anthropogenic
tract
tract: standard unit headword
#56 Sentence Equivalence
The university’s once ______ department of economic history has lost prestige and transmogrified into a department of management and marketing.The university’s once ______ department of economic history has lost prestige and transmogrified into a department of management and marketing.
答案：A, E　respected; venerable
venerable
venerable: standard unit headword
#57 Text Completion
The (i)______ nature of the candidate’s comments is calculated. As a long-standing target of critics who regard him as a radical, he understands that he needs to be as (ii)______ as possible if he is to overcome those critiques and appear as a (iii)______ leader.The (i)______ nature of the candidate’s comments is calculated. As a long-standing target of critics who regard him as a radical, he understands that he needs to be as (ii)______ as possible if he is to overcome those critiques and appear as a (iii)______ leader.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　platitudinous; innocuous; conciliatory
supercilious
supercilious: standard unit headword
#58 Sentence Equivalence
In a field of egotists, Bloomfield is ______, always praising her competitors and punctuating her correspondence with self-deprecating remarks.In a field of egotists, Bloomfield is ______, always praising her competitors and punctuating her correspondence with self-deprecating remarks.
答案：A, F　unassuming; modest
cutting
cutting: standard unit headword
#59 Text Completion
Researchers note that wolves’ otherwise strongly hierarchical society is marked by occasional displays of populist (i)______: if a pack leader proves a too-snappish tyrant, subordinate wolves will (ii)______ the top cur.Researchers note that wolves’ otherwise strongly hierarchical society is marked by occasional displays of populist (i)______: if a pack leader proves a too-snappish tyrant, subordinate wolves will (ii)______ the top cur.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　umbrage; collectively overthrow
overthrow
overthrow: standard unit headword
#60 Sentence Equivalence
Films that critics have slumbered through rarely generate industry excitement, even though the critics’ ______ reception may be less the fault of the movie than of its unfortunate time slot near a fatiguing film festival’s conclusion.Films that critics have slumbered through rarely generate industry excitement, even though the critics’ ______ reception may be less the fault of the movie than of its unfortunate time slot near a fatiguing film festival’s conclusion.
答案：A, C　somnolent; lethargic
laconic
laconic: standard unit headword
#61 Text Completion
The author of this travel guide (i)______ to show readers Cairo as it really is, but his information is not reliable. For instance, his geography is (ii)______, with one walking tour covering areas of the city that are twenty miles apart.The author of this travel guide (i)______ to show readers Cairo as it really is, but his information is not reliable. For instance, his geography is (ii)______, with one walking tour covering areas of the city that are twenty miles apart.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　purports; erratic
forbear
forbear: standard unit headword
#62 Sentence Equivalence
When the atmosphere over the city is at its best, it is peculiarly ______, and this clarity seems to distill the very special beauty of the place.When the atmosphere over the city is at its best, it is peculiarly ______, and this clarity seems to distill the very special beauty of the place.
答案：A, E　limpid; pellucid
limpid
limpid: standard unit headword
#63 Text Completion
The historian of ancient science Otto Neugebauer concluded that Babylonian astronomical texts are (i)______ because everything has been eliminated from the astronomy except observations and the mathematical consequences of an initial hypothesis about the fundamental character of the astronomical movements. This judgment cohered with the high level of mathematical theory, which (ii)______ mathematical computation together with empirical observation as (iii)______ of science and denied any role to speculative hypotheses of a strongly theoretical nature.The historian of ancient science Otto Neugebauer concluded that Babylonian astronomical texts are (i)______ because everything has been eliminated from the astronomy except observations and the mathematical consequences of an initial hypothesis about the fundamental character of the astronomical movements. This judgment cohered with the high level of mathematical theory, which (ii)______ mathematical computation together with empirical observation as (iii)______ of science and denied any role to speculative hypotheses of a strongly theoretical nature.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　scientific; admitted; necessary characteristic
end result
end result: standard unit headword
#64 Sentence Equivalence
Despite a tendency to be overtly ______, the poetry does not consist solely of pious sentiments: it sparks the imagination and provides lively entertainment.Despite a tendency to be overtly ______, the poetry does not consist solely of pious sentiments: it sparks the imagination and provides lively entertainment.
答案：A, D　preachy; sanctimonious
plaintive
plaintive: standard unit headword
#65 Text Completion
The professor frequently reiterated a basic assumption behind the experimental method—namely, that the outcome of the experiment is always (i)______. The hypothesis can never assume the experiment’s results, in other words, but instead must (ii)______ their appearance.The professor frequently reiterated a basic assumption behind the experimental method—namely, that the outcome of the experiment is always (i)______. The hypothesis can never assume the experiment’s results, in other words, but instead must (ii)______ their appearance.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　undetermined; await
negate

Though the play crackles with humor, the dialogue is less (i)______ when it comes to the drama’s emotional core. There the players tend to spell out their emotions in (ii)______ aphorisms, and repeat them as necessary.Though the play crackles with humor, the dialogue is less (i)______ when it comes to the drama’s emotional core. There the players tend to spell out their emotions in (ii)______ aphorisms, and repeat them as necessary.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　adroit; jejune

答案：B　irascible
perfidious

#68 Text Completion
The guilty-pleasure of the play lies in its invitation for us to identify with its characters—attractive, articulate young women and young men (i)______ behavior that, from the outside, may look more than a little (ii)______ : though the actions are initially repugnant, we cannot help but share the point of the view of the characters committing them.The guilty-pleasure of the play lies in its invitation for us to identify with its characters—attractive, articulate young women and young men (i)______ behavior that, from the outside, may look more than a little (ii)______ : though the actions are initially repugnant, we cannot help but share the point of the view of the characters committing them.

Blank 2

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　duplicative; muddies

#74 TC DOUBLE
Weeds lack the lethality of insects and microbes, which can strike farm crops seemingly out of nowhere and rapidly wipe them out. It is worth bearing in mind, however, that infestations of insects and disease are typically (i)________ events, whereas weeds are (ii)________.Weeds lack the lethality of insects and microbes, which can strike farm crops seemingly out of nowhere and rapidly wipe them out. It is worth bearing in mind, however, that infestations of insects and disease are typically (i)________ events, whereas weeds are (ii)________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　sporadic; ubiquitous
strike
strike: standard unit headword
#75 TC DOUBLE
That there were a dozen complex debates on the lawyer's mind at any given moment did not (i) __________ his ability to perform physical work; on the contrary, he labored more vigorously when simultaneously engaged in such (ii) __________ forensic exercises.That there were a dozen complex debates on the lawyer's mind at any given moment did not (i) __________ his ability to perform physical work; on the contrary, he labored more vigorously when simultaneously engaged in such (ii) __________ forensic exercises.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　impede; cerebral
labor
labor: standard unit headword
#76 TC DOUBLE
Conservationists tend to be (i) ____________ framing their case in terms of personal feelings; if pushed to describe the moral roots of their convictions, they generally give (ii) ____________ arguments for species preservation, claiming that diversity is intrinsic to ecosystem health, which is in turn intrinsic to human survival.Conservationists tend to be (i) ____________ framing their case in terms of personal feelings; if pushed to describe the moral roots of their convictions, they generally give (ii) ____________ arguments for species preservation, claiming that diversity is intrinsic to ecosystem health, which is in turn intrinsic to human survival.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　uncomfortable with; utilitarian
utilitarian
utilitarian: standard unit headword

Ultimately, the young idealists (i)__________ the society they so fervently desired to reform: in their frustration, they reacted to the immediate situation by removing themselves from the postwar social and political arena, where the future of their country was being decided. In doing so, they (ii)_________ the salutary effects their rebellion should have (iii)____________ .Ultimately, the young idealists (i)__________ the society they so fervently desired to reform: in their frustration, they reacted to the immediate situation by removing themselves from the postwar social and political arena, where the future of their country was being decided. In doing so, they (ii)_________ the salutary effects their rebellion should have (iii)____________ .

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　did a great disservice to; nullified; begotten

immediate: standard unit headword

Strayed's advice columns were a clear step away from the_______ of societal norms by earlier columnists, instead, her approach was one of liberating, nonjudgmental permissiveness.Strayed's advice columns were a clear step away from the_______ of societal norms by earlier columnists, instead, her approach was one of liberating, nonjudgmental permissiveness.
答案：D, F　reinforcement; bolstering

norm: standard unit headword

答案：C　capricious

#80 TC TRIPLE
History is about recognizing continuities and therefore the historian must (i) __________ stories that rope together the disparate worlds of past and present. But it is also true that honest history arises from a sense of (ii) __________. It is the experience of discontinuity that alerts us to the (iii) __________ of the past and sets us looking for narratives to capture that unfamiliar entity.History is about recognizing continuities and therefore the historian must (i) __________ stories that rope together the disparate worlds of past and present. But it is also true that honest history arises from a sense of (ii) __________. It is the experience of discontinuity that alerts us to the (iii) __________ of the past and sets us looking for narratives to capture that unfamiliar entity.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, H　weave; interruptedness; strangeness
disparate

It is hard to write a good novel that takes a strong stand on a social issue, although there are a few novelists, Zola and Norris for example, who do manage to (i)_________ the often circumscribing effects of (ii)_______.In writing about environmental issues, the author takes on some special artistic challenges. It is hard to write a good novel that takes a strong stand on a social issue, although there are a few novelists, Zola and Norris for example, who do manage to (i)_________ the often circumscribing effects of (ii)_______.

Blank 2
答案：C, E　transcend; polemic

Now, however, historians have realized that in the mid-twentieth century the phrase "fundamental research" was sometimes (iii) __________ industrial scientists, who applied the phrase to their own work in order to distinguish it from that of applied scientists and thus elevate their status.Until recently, historians of science treated fundamental research as (i) __________ pure science--that is, both were taken to refer to scientific investigations (ii) __________ technological and/or commercial objectives. Now, however, historians have realized that in the mid-twentieth century the phrase "fundamental research" was sometimes (iii) __________ industrial scientists, who applied the phrase to their own work in order to distinguish it from that of applied scientists and thus elevate their status.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　synonymous with; free from; appropriated by

Blank 2

#104 TC TRIPLE
Thorough cleanings or restorations that try to return much-loved old master paintings to their original condition (i) __________. Quite apart from a reasonable wariness of paint loss, sheer loyalty to the (ii) __________ look of a work, dirt and all, prejudices many. There are also academics who (iii) __________ the removal of retouches and even of overpaintings, holding that even though they may date from years after the original composition, they embody history in themselves.Thorough cleanings or restorations that try to return much-loved old master paintings to their original condition (i) __________. Quite apart from a reasonable wariness of paint loss, sheer loyalty to the (ii) __________ look of a work, dirt and all, prejudices many. There are also academics who (iii) __________ the removal of retouches and even of overpaintings, holding that even though they may date from years after the original composition, they embody history in themselves.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　invariably face opposition; accustomed; militate against
pristine
pristine: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　undercut; harder to maintain

#106 SE
China has a long tradition of __________ the written word; whereas in Renaissance Europe, painting, sculpture, and architecture were viewed as the apex of artistic achievement, in China calligraphy was the preeminent art form.China has a long tradition of __________ the written word; whereas in Renaissance Europe, painting, sculpture, and architecture were viewed as the apex of artistic achievement, in China calligraphy was the preeminent art form.
答案：B, F　venerating; exalting
venerate
venerate: standard unit headword
#107 TC SINGLE
The evidence for impact cratering events within the solar system is __________: almost every solid planet or natural satellite whose surface has been observed with high enough resolution shows unmistakable evidence of impact craters.The evidence for impact cratering events within the solar system is __________: almost every solid planet or natural satellite whose surface has been observed with high enough resolution shows unmistakable evidence of impact craters.
答案：B　omnipresent
circumstantial
circumstantial: standard unit headword
#108 TC TRIPLE
A favorite tactic of George Eliot's irresponsibly (i) __________ biographer is to hoist an alluring psychological flag concerning Eliot and then scrupulously take it down again, because the evidence will not serve to (ii) __________. But she takes it down only after it has had its entirely (iii) __________ flutter in the breeze.A favorite tactic of George Eliot's irresponsibly (i) __________ biographer is to hoist an alluring psychological flag concerning Eliot and then scrupulously take it down again, because the evidence will not serve to (ii) __________. But she takes it down only after it has had its entirely (iii) __________ flutter in the breeze.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　speculative; keep it up; gratuitous
discount
discount: standard unit headword
#109 SE
By avoiding hyperbole in her definitive account of one of the most influential but controversial banks of the modern era, Hurst shows herself to be a __________ author.By avoiding hyperbole in her definitive account of one of the most influential but controversial banks of the modern era, Hurst shows herself to be a __________ author.
答案：A, D　judicious; sensible
judicious
judicious: standard unit headword
#110 TC SINGLE
The environmentalist asserted that although the trend in building regulations was theoretically geared toward energy efficiency, in terms of energy consumption, new buildings typically were becoming more __________ every year.The environmentalist asserted that although the trend in building regulations was theoretically geared toward energy efficiency, in terms of energy consumption, new buildings typically were becoming more __________ every year.
答案：C　profligate
capricious
capricious: standard unit headword
#111 SE
Although there is clear and direct evidence for the use of domestic horses in the Bronze Age cultures of the second millennium B.C.E., evidence for the use of horses before this period is ____________.Although there is clear and direct evidence for the use of domestic horses in the Bronze Age cultures of the second millennium B.C.E., evidence for the use of horses before this period is ____________.
答案：A, E　equivocal; ambiguous

circumstantial: standard unit headword
#112 TC TRIPLE
While many tools can automatically locate patterns in large data sets, they cannot yet distinguish truly significant patterns from those that are (i) __________. Thus statisticians, who by virtue of their training are (ii) __________ identifying (iii) __________ interpretations of patterns in data, must increasingly bear the burden of scrutinizing data and challenging conclusions based on statistical flukes.While many tools can automatically locate patterns in large data sets, they cannot yet distinguish truly significant patterns from those that are (i) __________. Thus statisticians, who by virtue of their training are (ii) __________ identifying (iii) __________ interpretations of patterns in data, must increasingly bear the burden of scrutinizing data and challenging conclusions based on statistical flukes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　merely fortuitous; adept at; spurious
adept
adept: standard unit headword
#113 TC SINGLE
The composer's music had been criticized for being simple and accessible to a fault, but defenders countered that music need not be __________ to be great.The composer's music had been criticized for being simple and accessible to a fault, but defenders countered that music need not be __________ to be great.
答案：C　inscrutable

capricious: standard unit headword

答案：B, D　doomed; moribund

capricious: standard unit headword

答案：D, F　paucity; dearth

#116 TC TRIPLE
The poem is not nearly as (i) __________ as it has often been said to be. Granted, it would be (ii) __________ to claim that the poem is self-contained or that its proliferating allusions are self-explanatory. But the poem is more self-contained than it appears when you read only fragments of it, and its allusions are often (iii) __________ by their contexts or by their repeated appearances in changing contexts.The poem is not nearly as (i) __________ as it has often been said to be. Granted, it would be (ii) __________ to claim that the poem is self-contained or that its proliferating allusions are self-explanatory. But the poem is more self-contained than it appears when you read only fragments of it, and its allusions are often (iii) __________ by their contexts or by their repeated appearances in changing contexts.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　difficult; absurd; explained
absurd
absurd: standard unit headword
#117 SE
Such is the strength of randomized controlled trials that the debate among medical researchers is now centered on whether there are any situations where such a trial is________.Such is the strength of randomized controlled trials that the debate among medical researchers is now centered on whether there are any situations where such a trial is________.
答案：B, C　unwarranted; inappropriate
onerous
onerous: standard unit headword

The story lines of silent dramas may often have been ______, yet within those basic narrative outlines, the true artists among silent-film actors could express emotional shadings that have no analogue in spoken language.The story lines of silent dramas may often have been ______, yet within those basic narrative outlines, the true artists among silent-film actors could express emotional shadings that have no analogue in spoken language.
答案：D, E　elemental; rudimentary

shading: standard unit headword
#119 SE
Although ants and humans have __________ lifestyles, they fight their foes for many of the same economic reasons, including access to dwelling spaces, to territory, and to food.Although ants and humans have __________ lifestyles, they fight their foes for many of the same economic reasons, including access to dwelling spaces, to territory, and to food.
答案：B, E　divergent; disparate

disparate: standard unit headword
#120 Sentence Equivalence

答案：D, F　rudimentary; sketchy
refer
refer: standard unit headword

答案：A, D　trump; supplant

答案：B, F　capricious; impulsive

Although penguins have a long history on the African continent, Africa has at times become __________ individual penguin species, with several species being driven to extinction before the Middle Pleistocene.Although penguins have a long history on the African continent, Africa has at times become __________ individual penguin species, with several species being driven to extinction before the Middle Pleistocene.
答案：B, E　untenable for; uninhabitable for

答案：B, F　indifference; apathy

#129 Text Completion
To get funding and tenured positions, medical researchers have to get their work published in well-regarded journals, where rejection can climb above 90 percent. Not surprisingly, the studies that tend to make the grade are those that make (i)______ claims. But while coming up with such (ii)______ claims is relatively easy, getting the data to bear them out is another matter. When studied rigorously, the great majority of these claims (iii)______.To get funding and tenured positions, medical researchers have to get their work published in well-regarded journals, where rejection can climb above 90 percent. Not surprisingly, the studies that tend to make the grade are those that make (i)______ claims. But while coming up with such (ii)______ claims is relatively easy, getting the data to bear them out is another matter. When studied rigorously, the great majority of these claims (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　eye-catching; striking; yield contradictory data

strike: standard unit headword | striking: standard unit headword

答案：B, F　a rigorous; a faithful

答案：C, F　rapacious; avaricious

答案：B, E　a hodgepodge; a patchwork

答案：A, C　inhospitable to; unreceptive to

With the grand ambition of sending unbreakable coded messages, some physicists are using exotic tools—quantum mechanics and streams of individual photos—to shut out (i)______. But a wire and a few resistors may (ii)______ a message as securely, according to a physicist who claims to have devised a simple and uncrackable scheme. The idea shows that more (iii)______ methods might compete with budding quantum cryptography.With the grand ambition of sending unbreakable coded messages, some physicists are using exotic tools—quantum mechanics and streams of individual photos—to shut out (i)______. But a wire and a few resistors may (ii)______ a message as securely, according to a physicist who claims to have devised a simple and uncrackable scheme. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　prying eyes; convey; mundane

答案：D, F　realism; verisimilitude

Blank 2
Blank 3

convey: standard unit headword
#162 Text Completion
People frequently attempt to relieve their workplace frustrations via surreptitious comments around the water-cooler but would be better able to resolve their resentments if they were less (i)______ about their problem and initiated a more (ii)______ dialogue.People frequently attempt to relieve their workplace frustrations via surreptitious comments around the water-cooler but would be better able to resolve their resentments if they were less (i)______ about their problem and initiated a more (ii)______ dialogue.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　clandestine; open
clandestine
clandestine: standard unit headword
#163 Sentence Equivalence
Williamson had a fierce commitment to achieving an accord, spending enormous amount of time trying to forge a consensus out of an often ______ assembly.Williamson had a fierce commitment to achieving an accord, spending enormous amount of time trying to forge a consensus out of an often ______ assembly.
答案：B, C　fractious; restive
forge
forge: standard unit headword

Although not enough to ______ the conventional view of the manuscript’s provenance, the new study was thought to have weakened the prevailing theory considerably.Although not enough to ______ the conventional view of the manuscript’s provenance, the new study was thought to have weakened the prevailing theory considerably.
答案：E　topple
considerably
considerably: standard unit headword

To keep the museum’s admission lines moving, security inspections are considerably ______.To keep the museum’s admission lines moving, security inspections are considerably ______.
答案：C, D　cursory; casual
considerably
considerably: standard unit headword
#166 Text Completion
Campus-wide discussion on academic integrity can be (i)______ by the fact that faculty and students tend to define cheating in (ii)______ ways. Even when they concur on what cheating means, faculty and students often assign different levels of severity to specific violations. These differences can serve as a major (iii)______ the creation of a commonly accepted set of standards of integrity that are consistently applied to all academic work within the campus community.Campus-wide discussion on academic integrity can be (i)______ by the fact that faculty and students tend to define cheating in (ii)______ ways. Even when they concur on what cheating means, faculty and students often assign different levels of severity to specific violations. These differences can serve as a major (iii)______ the creation of a commonly accepted set of standards of integrity that are consistently applied to all academic work within the campus community.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　hampered; disparate; impediment to
disparate
disparate: standard unit headword

答案：C, D　castigating; chastising

distort: standard unit headword
#168 Text Completion
Although strikes remain rarer in Britain than in many other European countries, and their economic impact is (i)______ compared to the great upheavals of the 1970s and 1980s, their number has (ii)______ after a few years of somewhat greater calm.Although strikes remain rarer in Britain than in many other European countries, and their economic impact is (i)______ compared to the great upheavals of the 1970s and 1980s, their number has (ii)______ after a few years of somewhat greater calm.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　negligible; revived

In Inuit culture, elaborate carving has often been used to enhance ______ objects such as harpoon heads and other tools.In Inuit culture, elaborate carving has often been used to enhance ______ objects such as harpoon heads and other tools.
答案：A, B　utilitarian; functional
utilitarian
utilitarian: standard unit headword

答案：D　galvanizes

答案：A, B　implacable; unyielding

Blank 2
答案：A, E　too harshly judged; judicious

答案：D, E　stubborn; intractable

The art of letter writing is like the art of acting in that it is the impression of (i)______ which usually makes a performance convincing. Great letter writers, like great actors, have a gift for (ii)______ , for the here and now, the depth of expression depending on its closeness to actual processes of thought. By these criteria Ellen Terry was a remarkable letter writer indeed: her letters invariably have an air of (iii)______ .The art of letter writing is like the art of acting in that it is the impression of (i)______ which usually makes a performance convincing. Great letter writers, like great actors, have a gift for (ii)______ , for the here and now, the depth of expression depending on its closeness to actual processes of thought. By these criteria Ellen Terry was a remarkable letter writer indeed: her letters invariably have an air of (iii)______ .

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　spontaneity; immediacy; improvisation

答案：A, B　singular; unique

答案：B　extirpate

Yet autobiography, even when ostensibly steeped in candor, tends toward (ii)______ through later perspectives, afterwords, and second thoughts, whereas letters have an undeniable (iii)______ , Offering select glimpses of the fraught and living moment.It is plausible to regard a collection of letters spanning youth and old age as (i)______ of autobiography: the precession of characters who inhabit a life and a chronology of incidents turn up reliably in either form. Yet autobiography, even when ostensibly steeped in candor, tends toward (ii)______ through later perspectives, afterwords, and second thoughts, whereas letters have an undeniable (iii)______ , Offering select glimpses of the fraught and living moment.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　an approximation; reconsideration; immediacy

Contrary to those who fear the impact of invasive species on native plants, the biologists contend that the threat posed to biodiversity by nonnative plants is often (i)______. For instance, a study of garlic mustard, a nonnative plant thriving in the forest, found that the garlic mustard abundance in forest plots is not (ii)______ the number of other species there.Contrary to those who fear the impact of invasive species on native plants, the biologists contend that the threat posed to biodiversity by nonnative plants is often (i)______. For instance, a study of garlic mustard, a nonnative plant thriving in the forest, found that the garlic mustard abundance in forest plots is not (ii)______ the number of other species there.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　exaggerated; related to
pose

For those of us who have been intoxicated by the power and potential of mathematics, the mystery isn’t why that fascination developed but why it isn’t ______.For those of us who have been intoxicated by the power and potential of mathematics, the mystery isn’t why that fascination developed but why it isn’t ______.
答案：C, F　prevalent; widespread
discount
discount: standard unit headword
#186 Text Completion
For Plato the art of music was so (i)______ moral and political reality that any alteration to music system would necessarily require a corresponding political shift. But two and a half millennia later, when classical music is generally seen merely as a lifestyle accessory, Plato’s conception seems (ii)______. To be sure, there are still people who consider classical music to be of (iii)______ cultural importance, but few of them are able to articulate this convincingly.For Plato the art of music was so (i)______ moral and political reality that any alteration to music system would necessarily require a corresponding political shift. But two and a half millennia later, when classical music is generally seen merely as a lifestyle accessory, Plato’s conception seems (ii)______. To be sure, there are still people who consider classical music to be of (iii)______ cultural importance, but few of them are able to articulate this convincingly.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　firmly anchored in; absurd; profound
absurd
absurd: standard unit headword

答案：D, F　proscriptive; exacting

#188 Text Completion
Evidence has been accumulating since the 1930s that reducing an animals energy intake below its energy expenditure extends the life span and delays the (i)______ of age-related diseases in rats, dogs, fish, and monkeys. Such results have inspired thousands of people to (ii)______ in the hope of living longer, healthier lives. They have also led to a search for drugs that (iii)______ the effects of calorie restriction without the pain of actually going on a diet.Evidence has been accumulating since the 1930s that reducing an animals energy intake below its energy expenditure extends the life span and delays the (i)______ of age-related diseases in rats, dogs, fish, and monkeys. Such results have inspired thousands of people to (ii)______ in the hope of living longer, healthier lives. They have also led to a search for drugs that (iii)______ the effects of calorie restriction without the pain of actually going on a diet.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　onset; put up with constant hunger; mimic
inspired
inspired: standard unit headword

Anna Gavalda’s fiction is appealing largely because of her writing styles, which is certainly (i)______. It is heavy on dialogue (and light on embellishment), features very short sections (of often only a few sentence) in rapid succession, and in general favors an unadorned directness. Not surprisingly, this style works better in short-story form than in novels, and the story collection I Wish Someone Were Waiting for me Somewhere (ii)______ her attempt to (iii)______ it in a novel, as in, for example, Someone I Loved.Anna Gavalda’s fiction is appealing largely because of her writing styles, which is certainly (i)______. It is heavy on dialogue (and light on embellishment), features very short sections (of often only a few sentence) in rapid succession, and in general favors an unadorned directness. Not surprisingly, this style works better in short-story form than in novels, and the story collection I Wish Someone Were Waiting for me Somewhere (ii)______ her attempt to (iii)______ it in a novel, as in, for example, Someone I Loved.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　distinctive; impresses considerably more than; sustain
considerably

One sometimes hears that Marco Polo introduced pasta to the Western world, having encountered it in China. This durable myth, which (i)______ that nothing should have been known of pasta in Italy until 1295, when Marco Polo returned from the Far East, can easily be (ii)______ by pointing out that there are Italian references to pasta that (iii)______ .One sometimes hears that Marco Polo introduced pasta to the Western world, having encountered it in China. This durable myth, which (i)______ that nothing should have been known of pasta in Italy until 1295, when Marco Polo returned from the Far East, can easily be (ii)______ by pointing out that there are Italian references to pasta that (iii)______ .
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　requires; debunked; predate that event

reference: standard unit headword
